task:
Redo the Foreman STATUSLINE and DASHBOARD so they show meaningful, live, session-aware info (repo: my-pi-harness, extension: extensions/foreman). The founder's complaint: today's footer statusline ("foreman  ✓ Phase D · done   ✓ Fix… · done   +24 more") is useless — it's a flat list dominated by finished tasks, labels say nothing, and across multiple concurrent sessions in one repo you can't tell which task belongs to which session.

READ FIRST (do real recon, cite file:line): extensions/foreman/dashboard/reader.ts (the PURE model: listTasks, readActivity, listRuns, readTranscript, buildRootRows, and the statusline pair buildStatuslineModel + formatStatusline at ~line 391/470), extensions/foreman/dashboard/view.ts (the ForemanDashboard TUI: picker → root → agent views), extensions/foreman/index.ts (the pushStatus()/spinner block at ~line 1042-1075 that calls ctx.ui.setStatus(STATUS_KEY,…), and the ctrl+b / ctrl+f shortcut handlers at ~line 950-975), extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh (the headless test that already covers the statusline model+format — this is your verify target to extend), and extensions/foreman/docs/DASHBOARD-SPEC.md (Phase C section).

VERIFIED PI API FACTS (installed pi 0.78.0 — use these, do not reinvent):
- ctx.ui.setStatus(key, text) is STRICTLY ONE LINE: pi's footer.js sanitizeStatusText strips \n\t\r to spaces then truncates to width. A bordered multi-line "FOREMAN" section is IMPOSSIBLE via setStatus.
- ctx.ui.setWidget(key, lines: string[] | undefined, { placement: "aboveEditor" }) renders a MULTI-LINE panel above the editor (up to 10 lines, full width, ANSI colors preserved; pass undefined to clear). THIS is the primitive for the bordered section. Verified at interactive-mode.js setExtensionWidget + types.d.ts:96.
- The ctrl+b/ctrl+f shortcut handler receives a full ExtensionContext; ctx.sessionManager.getSessionId() returns the current session id (session-manager.d.ts:188). The foreman tool already has sessionId via ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionId?.() at index.ts:996.
- Transcript JSONL events carry: agent_start {t, role, round, model, task}, tool_call {t, name, args}, tool_result {t, name, ok, preview}, usage {t, input, output, cost, contextTokens}, agent_end. So per live task you can derive elapsed (now − agent_start.t), current action (last tool_call), tool count (#tool_call), and ctx tokens (last usage.contextTokens) — all pure file reads.

DELIVERABLE 1 — STATUSLINE → a branded, bordered, session-scoped, LIVE panel (replace the one-line footer with a setWidget panel):
A) In reader.ts, ENRICH the statusline model. Add to each StatuslineTask (or a new buildStatusPanelModel) for RUNNING tasks: liveAction (human string from the last tool_call of the active transcript: bash→"running <cmd≤32>", read→"reading <basename>", write/edit→"editing <basename>", grep/find→"searching", planner/tester/reviewer phases keep their role word, else the tool name), toolCount (#tool_call in active transcript), ctxTokens (last usage.contextTokens), elapsedMs (now − agent_start.t). Keep it PURE (inject now), defensive (missing/truncated transcript ⇒ omit the field, never throw), and read only the active transcript (cap reads; a 50KB file is fine).
B) In reader.ts, add formatStatusPanel(model, opts): string[] returning the bordered panel lines (injectable color + spinner frame + now, same testability as formatStatusline). Exact target (match this taste; ≤7 lines, colors via the injected colorizer using existing theme tokens accent/warning/success/muted/dim/text/error):
   ─ FOREMAN ───────────────────────────────  2 active · 12 done
   ⠙ DEV   Phase C reviewer · R2/3 · 1m12s
     ↳ editing index.ts · 18 tools · 44k ctx
   ◆ Phase B planner — needs you: ship?
   ⚠ Fix the planner timeout — stuck
   Rules: line 1 is a title rule "─ FOREMAN ─…" left, with a right-aligned "<N active> · <N done>" summary (omit a zero side). Then ONE block per non-done task in ATTENTION order (running first, then gates awaiting you [awaiting_ship→"needs you: ship?", planning→"needs you: plan?"], then escalated→"stuck"). The running task gets the animated braille spinner (accent) + a role badge (DEV/VERIFY/TEST/PLAN/REVIEW from phase, accent), the task label (text), "R<round>/<max> · <elapsed>" (muted, elapsed as Xm Ys or Ys), AND an indented dim sub-line "↳ <liveAction> · <toolCount> tools · <ctx>k ctx" (omit pieces that are unknown). Gate tasks: "◆ <label> — needs you: ship?/plan?" (warning glyph). Escalated: "⚠ <label> — stuck" (error/warning). Finished tasks are NOT listed individually — they ONLY contribute to the "· N done" count in the title (this is the founder's "Collapse to count"). If >N (say 4) non-done blocks, show the first 4 and a "+K more" muted line. Label = the existing shortLabel head logic (reuse it). Keep the brand uppercase "FOREMAN".
C) reader.ts must keep buildStatuslineModel session-scoping (opts.sessionId) — the panel is built ONLY from this session's tasks (ownerSessionId === sessionId), exactly as today.
D) In index.ts, REPLACE the single-line setStatus(STATUS_KEY,…) footer with the panel: build the enriched model (sessionId-scoped) and call ctx.ui.setWidget(STATUS_KEY, lines, { placement: "aboveEditor" }) on every phase transition and on the ~120ms spinner ticker (so the spinner animates and elapsed/liveAction update live). When the session has NO non-done tasks (nothing running/gating/stuck), call ctx.ui.setWidget(STATUS_KEY, undefined) to CLEAR the panel (no idle clutter). Keep the no-UI guard (typeof ctx.ui.setWidget !== "function" ⇒ no-op, so headless/RPC is unaffected). Render elapsed at second granularity so the ~8Hz ticker doesn't jitter the text. Leave the DIRECT_STATUS_KEY direct-edit-mode status exactly as-is.

DELIVERABLE 2 — DASHBOARD → make session ownership unmistakable (founder runs multiple sessions per repo and can't tell tasks apart):
A) Thread the current sessionId into the dashboard: in the ctrl+b/ctrl+f handlers pass ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionId?.() into new ForemanDashboard(ctx.cwd, tui, theme, done, { openLive?, sessionId }). Store it on the component.
B) PICKER view (renderPicker/renderTaskRow): add an OWNER BADGE to every task row — "● yours" (accent) when task.ownerSessionId === this.sessionId, "session <first6 of id>" (dim) when owned by another session, "—" (dim) when no owner (legacy). Update the picker HEADER to show "<N> tasks · <K> yours". SORT the picker by attention then ownership: primary = attention rank (running/live > awaiting_ship/planning gate > escalated > in_progress > done), secondary = yours-before-others, tertiary = updatedAt desc (keep listTasks’ pure sort as-is but add a view-level comparator, OR add an exported pure sortForPicker(tasks, sessionId) in reader.ts and unit-test it). Add a footer hint + a 'm' key that toggles a MINE-ONLY filter (show only this session's tasks); reflect the filter state in the header ("mine only").
C) ROOT (mission-control) header: show the owner badge for that task too ("● yours" / "session abc123").
D) AGENT view header: enrich the live header using the same derived fields — show elapsed and the current liveAction (e.g. "● running · editing index.ts · 1m12s · 18 tools") next to role/round/model, so the live agent view reads as truly live. Reuse the reader enrichment; don't duplicate logic.
Keep all existing dashboard navigation/keys working (picker↔root↔agent, live tailing/follow, ctrl+b/ctrl+f toggle-close). Read-only over the ledger — do NOT change the loop, gates, verdict logic, ledger schema, activity.json writers, or crew/*.md.

STRICT CONSTRAINTS:
- Minimal blast radius: only reader.ts, view.ts, index.ts (statusline wiring + dashboard sessionId), and the reader_test.sh. No changes to the foreman loop control flow, gates.ts, ledger.ts schema/writers, guard.ts, planner/reviewer/ship, or any crew/*.md.
- reader.ts stays PURE (node-builtins only, NO pi/TUI imports) so it unit-tests headlessly. All new model/format/sort helpers go in reader.ts and are pure + injectable (now, color, frame).
- Defensive: every new reader function tolerates missing/empty/truncated files and returns safe defaults, never throws. The headless path (no setWidget) must be a clean no-op.
- Quota safety and the append-only crew spawning are untouched (you don't go near runAgent).
- Do not add features beyond the two deliverables. No new colors beyond the existing theme tokens. Keep the panel ≤7 lines (setWidget caps at 10).

TEST (verify target): EXTEND extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh (same headless node --input-type=module + node:assert/strict style, NO TTY, NO model spawn). Add assertions for: (1) the enriched statusline model on a RUNNING task derives liveAction/toolCount/ctxTokens/elapsedMs from a seeded active transcript (seed agent_start with a fixed t + a couple tool_call + a usage), and omits those fields when the transcript is absent; (2) formatStatusPanel output: first line starts with "─ FOREMAN", includes the "N active · M done" summary with done COLLAPSED to a count (a seeded done task must NOT appear as its own block), a running task renders the spinner+role badge+elapsed+the "↳ … tools … ctx" sub-line, a gate task renders "needs you: ship?", and the spinner differs across frame 0 vs 1; (3) session scoping: a task owned by another session is excluded from the panel; (4) if you add sortForPicker, assert attention+yours-first ordering (running before gate before done; yours before others at equal attention). Keep ALL existing reader_test assertions green, and keep the other fast suites passing.

VERIFY COMMAND: bash extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/ledger_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/gates_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/fallback_test.sh

End with the mandatory DEV-JSON machine block.
verifyCommand: bash extensions/foreman/dashboard/test/reader_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/ledger_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/gates_test.sh && bash extensions/foreman/test/fallback_test.sh
